definitions: An elected official or group continuing in office during the period between an election defeat and the inauguration of a successor.
A person or thing that is weak, ineffectual, or unable to cope successfully.
A stockbroker who cannot meet their obligations.
examples: The president was a lame duck after losing the election.
The company was a lame duck, struggling to innovate.
Investors worried about the lame duck on the trading floor.
